headline: First-ever evidence-based baseline: Norway formally restricts temporary collective protection for Ukrainian men aged 18-60 (announced Feb 25, formalized March 27, applied to applications from May 5, 2026); separate refugee-welfare consultation (late-April deadline) replaces social-assistance and housing benefits with lower unified integration allowance; age-and-sex restriction is novel discriminatory layer in Nordic refugee context
methodology_notes: First-ever evidence-based baseline (rotation-state showed null last_assessed). Live published composite 84.7, rank 6 (NOT 100/ceiling artifact as flagged in scanner — see data-integrity flag). The published scores are evidence-based Exemplary substrate, not a ceiling artifact. Primary anchor: March 27, 2026 government announcement that Ukrainian men aged 18-60 will no longer automatically receive temporary collective protection (TCP) — applications received on or after May 5, 2026 are processed under standard asylum procedure on a case-by-case basis. The categorical exclusion based jointly on age and sex is the discriminatory layer: it singles out a population subset (military-age males) for procedural disadvantage in a humanitarian-protection scheme. Secondary anchor: late-April 2026 government consultation on replacing standard social-assistance and housing benefits for refugees with a lower unified integration allowance for up to five years. EQU drops most sharply (-13.7) — categorical age-and-sex discrimination in humanitarian protection is a structural EQU event; the population subject to differential treatment includes individuals fleeing conscription that may itself constitute persecution. INT drops (-5.0) reflecting policy direction toward narrowing protections that contradicts Norway's stated humanitarian-leadership posture. BND modestly down (-7.5) — narrower protection scope reduces the boundary of state responsibility toward a vulnerable group. SYS/ACT minor reductions (-6.2 each) reflect the welfare-cut consultation. Norway retains genuine Exemplary substrate (universal healthcare, social democracy, gender equality across population, low corruption) supporting Established-band 81.3 floor across most dimensions. Composite 78.1 places Norway in the Established band (just below 80 boundary), consistent with the calibration tier of Denmark (81.3 corrected on April 25). Delta -6.6 is above 5-point threshold; medium confidence reflects (1) the protection ban applies to a specific subpopulation (Ukrainian conscripted-age men) rather than to refugees broadly, (2) the welfare-cut consultation is mid-process with outcome uncertain, and (3) Norway's broader institutional substrate remains exemplary. Calibration reference: Denmark (-18.7) and Sweden (-15.9) corrections were larger because each country had multiple compounding triggers; Norway's one in-window event with one secondary signal warrants a more contained delta.

evidence.claim: Norway officially announced March 27, 2026 that men aged 18-60 will no longer automatically receive temporary collective protection. Change applies to applications received on or after May 5, 2026. Single fathers and individuals already holding refugee status are exempted.

Initial February 25, 2026 announcement: Justice Minister Astri Aas-Hansen confirmed Norway will stop accepting Ukrainian men of conscription age as TCP recipients. Decision rationale cites strain on local services and housing due to ~100,000 displaced Ukrainians.

Norway government consultation (late April 2026) on replacing social assistance and housing benefits for refugees with a single, lower integration allowance for up to five years; consultation deadline within April 2026 window.
Norden policy framework documentation: Norway's evolving migrant integration policy 2025 — context for the April 2026 specific restrictions.

Genuine Exemplary substrate: universal healthcare, social democratic welfare model, gender equality leadership, low corruption (CPI top tier), strong labour rights via Working Environment Act, free education through tertiary level. Supports Established-band 81.3 floor across dimensions.
UDI (Norwegian Directorate of Immigration) protection-application page documents the procedural shift for asylum vs. TCP applicants.

key_evidence: March 27, 2026: Norway formally bans Ukrainian men aged 18-60 from automatic temporary collective protection; effective for applications May 5, 2026 onward
Categorical age-and-sex exclusion in humanitarian protection — novel discriminatory layer
Late-April 2026 welfare-cut consultation: lower unified integration allowance to replace social-assistance and housing benefits
~100,000 Ukrainian refugees in Norway — context for policy rationale (service-strain framing)
Genuine Exemplary substrate (healthcare, gender equality, social democracy) supports Established 81.3 floor
First-ever evidence-based baseline; rotation-state had null last_assessed
